headline: Your engineering waterfall journey.
subheadline: A scrollable GTMx strategy artifact for routing US mid-market operators into the right outcome-owned engineering offer.
productSummary: Technovate.One sells engineering as a single accountable partner: cloud and DevOps, data engineering and analytics, practical AI, product engineering, ERP implementation, and legacy platform re-engineering.
coreInsight: Technovate’s proof is strongest in specific rooms: eyecare and optical, jewellery and gemology, specialty manufacturing, PE-backed multi-location roll-ups, and operators with fragmented systems and data.

sections.label: Signal
sections.title: The signal is visible engineering pressure
sections.takeaway: Hiring, tech-stack, acquisition, funding, and AI/data language reveal which engineering problem is most urgent.

sections.label: Waterfall
sections.title: One cascade routes every vertical-match account
sections.takeaway: Every account enters the strongest observable tier first: active build-out, modernization, data/AI readiness, or vertical catch-all.

sections.label: Tiers
sections.title: Each tier auditions Technovate’s ownership model
sections.takeaway: The lead magnet is not a brochure. It is a small piece of the work Technovate sells, delivered before the call.

sections.label: Campaigns
sections.title: Proof-led angles for specific rooms
sections.takeaway: Eyecare, PE roll-ups, manufacturing, jewellery, and legacy ERP signals create more credible hooks than generic dev-shop copy.

sections.label: Email Copy
sections.title: Copy that proves research before the ask
sections.takeaway: Each email starts with a scraped observation, names the inferred pain, then offers a concrete teardown or benchmark.

sections.label: Launch
sections.title: Launch highest-intent plus strongest proof first
sections.takeaway: Start with active build-out and eyecare lookalikes, then phase in modernization, data/AI readiness, and the catch-all.

signalReasons: Open engineering clusters prove budget, urgency, and roadmap pressure.
Legacy stacks, ERP/POS fingerprints, PE ownership, and acquisitions reveal modernization pain.
AI and data-transformation language without a data platform reveals readiness gaps.
Vertical proof gives Technovate a sharper story than a generic engineering-services pitch.

sources.name: Apollo / LinkedIn Sales Navigator
sources.delivers: TAM spine, vertical, headcount, US geo, and decision-maker contacts

sources.name: LinkedIn Jobs / Indeed
sources.delivers: Open engineering, data, cloud, DevOps, ERP, and product roles

sources.name: BuiltWith / Wappalyzer
sources.delivers: Legacy stack, ERP/POS fingerprints, platform detection, and cloud maturity

sources.name: Crunchbase / PitchBook + PE pages
sources.delivers: PE backing, funding, recent acquisitions, and roll-up signals

sources.name: Company news / careers pages
sources.delivers: AI, data transformation, new location, and digital transformation language

sources.name: Vertical directories
sources.delivers: Eyecare groups, jewellery associations, manufacturing registries, and niche lookalike pools

routingRules.if: vertical_match AND (legacy_stack_flag OR erp_pos_fingerprint = legacy OR pe_backed_flag OR recent_acquisition_flag)
vertical_match AND ai_data_ambition_signal AND has_data_platform_flag = false

tiers.name: Tier 1
tiers.label: Active Build-Out
tiers.trigger: Vertical-match account with 3+ open engineering roles, oldest open 30+ days
tiers.pain: Roadmap is approved but staffing is lagging, and the fallback is stitching together freelancers or contractors.
tiers.valueProp: A ONE Pod plugs in a full accountable team in weeks, owning the outcome from idea to production.

tiers.name: Tier 2
tiers.label: Legacy and ERP Modernization
tiers.trigger: Legacy stack, ERP/POS fingerprint, PE-backed ownership, or recent acquisition
tiers.pain: A brittle legacy platform or fragmented systems across acquired sites is blocking clean reporting and operations.
tiers.valueProp: Technovate owns platform re-engineering, ERP modernization, and data consolidation end to end.

tiers.name: Tier 3
tiers.label: Data and AI Readiness
tiers.trigger: AI/data ambition signal without visible data platform
tiers.pain: Leadership wants AI and clean analytics, but data is siloed across ERP, CRM, apps, and spreadsheets.
tiers.valueProp: Technovate connects the data foundation first, ships early dashboards, then layers practical AI on top.

tiers.name: Tier 4
tiers.label: Vertical Catch-All
tiers.trigger: Any remaining account in a proven Technovate vertical
tiers.pain: Engineering is a bottleneck, often spread across freelancers and vendors with no owner of the whole.
tiers.valueProp: One partner from idea to production, no handoffs and no finger-pointing.

campaigns.name: Active Build-Out
campaigns.strategy: Use open roles, oldest role age, and job-stack keywords
campaigns.value: Start a full team in weeks
campaigns.overview: The strongest in-market signal. Show build-vs-hire cost and speed from their actual openings.

campaigns.name: Legacy and ERP Modernization
campaigns.filter: Legacy stack, ERP/POS, PE-backed, or recent acquisition
campaigns.value: Modernize without making it the internal team's nights-and-weekends project
campaigns.overview: The highest-ACV tier, anchored in Technovate's legacy POS, ERP, and PE-backed consolidation proof.

campaigns.name: Data and AI Readiness
campaigns.filter: AI/data ambition but no data platform
campaigns.strategy: Compare public ambition against visible foundation
campaigns.value: Production-grade AI after the data is ready
campaigns.overview: Educational but resonant for companies saying AI out loud before their data is clean.

campaigns.name: Vertical Engineering Benchmark
campaigns.strategy: Use vertical proof and benchmark report
campaigns.value: One accountable partner from idea to production
campaigns.overview: The evergreen volume campaign by vertical: eyecare, jewellery, manufacturing, and more.

campaigns.name: Eyecare / Optical Lookalike
campaigns.filter: US multi-location eyecare and optical groups, 10+ locations
campaigns.strategy: Reference EDW and legacy POS to Acuity Logic proof
campaigns.value: Vertical-exact proof nobody else can easily claim
campaigns.overview: The strongest side campaign because Technovate's optical proof is unusually specific.

campaigns.name: PE Portfolio Standardization
campaigns.filter: PE operating partners with multi-location services roll-ups
campaigns.strategy: Pitch the PE firm, not only the portco
campaigns.value: Standardize engineering and data across the portfolio
campaigns.overview: A higher-leverage buyer path for roll-up systems consolidation.

campaigns.name: ERP End-of-Life
campaigns.value: Own the migration before support ends

campaigns.name: Just Acquired
campaigns.overview: Acquisition timing turns systems fragmentation into urgent work.

campaigns.name: New CTO / VP Eng / VP Data
campaigns.value: Scoped pod delivers in first quarter
campaigns.overview: A leadership-change campaign for quick, credible wins.

campaigns.name: Recently Funded
campaigns.strategy: Growth pressure before engineering capacity catches up

campaigns.name: Hiring a First Data Hire
campaigns.value: Build foundation, then the hire runs it
campaigns.overview: A practical wedge into data engineering.

campaigns.name: New Location / New Plant
campaigns.filter: Announced new site, store, or facility
campaigns.strategy: Systems load jumps at the next site

campaigns.name: Manufacturing Ops
campaigns.value: Manufacturing ops platform, not marketing site
campaigns.overview: Anchored in PGP Glass, Croda, Barco, and pharma ops proof.

campaigns.name: Jewellery / Gemology
campaigns.filter: Jewellers and gem labs like Suvarna Kala and EGL
campaigns.overview: Small pool, but a distinctive wedge.

campaigns.name: Trade-Show Roster
campaigns.filter: Vision Expo, JCK, pharma and manufacturing expos
campaigns.strategy: Event roster plus vertical systems gap
campaigns.overview: Warm follow-up around shared event context.

campaigns.name: Legacy-Stack Alert
campaigns.value: Risk and phased path off it

campaigns.name: Vendor Fragmentation
campaigns.strategy: You are the integrator by default

messages.subject: {{company}} is hiring {{open_eng_roles_count}} engineers
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company}} has {{open_eng_roles_count}} engineering roles open, and the {{oldest_role_title}} one has been up for {{oldest_open_role_age_days}} days.

Would it be fair to assume the roadmap is waiting on those seats to fill?

We run purpose-built pods, a full team that owns an outcome and starts in weeks, not the three to six months it takes to recruit a squad. Unlike staff-aug, we own delivery from idea to production.

Reply Y and I'll send a short teardown: what those {{open_eng_roles_count}} hires cost fully-loaded vs the same scope shipped by a pod. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, one thing I should have led with: a pod is not bodies for hire.

One partner owns the whole thing, so you are not managing five contractors and refereeing whose bug it is.

That is the difference between renting hands and handing off an outcome.

Want the teardown on your {{open_eng_roles_count}} open roles? Reply Y.

messages.subject: teardown for {{company}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no need for a call.

The teardown shows what your open engineering roles cost fully-loaded vs a pod that ships the same scope, and how fast each gets to production.

Should I send it to you, or is {{eng_leader_name}} the right person for hiring vs build?

messages.subject: {{company}}'s systems after the acquisitions
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, noticed {{company}} is {{pe_or_growth_context}} and has grown by acquisition. In our experience that leaves each site on its own POS, ERP, or CRM, and no single source of truth for reporting.

Would it be fair to assume pulling one clean number across all locations is still painful?

We consolidate that into one reliable data and systems layer, owned end to end. We did exactly this for a PE-backed multi-location services group and a large US eyecare organization.

Reply Y and I'll send a one-page consolidation blueprint for {{company}}. Free.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the reason these projects stall is not budget.

It is that no internal owner wants to hold a risky multi-quarter migration on top of their day job.

That is the whole point of how we work. One partner owns the problem, not just the scope. If it slips, that is on us.

Want the blueprint for {{company}}? Reply Y.

messages.subject: modernization blueprint
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no pitch.

The blueprint is a one-page target-state architecture and a phased path to get there from what {{company}} runs today.

Should I send it to you, or is {{it_leader_name}} the right owner for systems and data?

messages.subject: {{company}} and AI
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company}} talking about {{ai_data_signal}}. Then looked at the stack and did not see a data platform underneath it.

Would it be fair to assume the data is still spread across the ERP, CRM, and a stack of spreadsheets that someone rebuilds every month?

We connect all of that into one reliable system and deliver in phases, so you get core dashboards early instead of waiting a year. Then AI on top, production-grade, not a demo.

Reply Y and I'll send a 10-point readiness scorecard for {{company}}.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the trap most teams fall into is buying an AI tool before the data is ready, then blaming the tool when the outputs are garbage.

Clean, connected data first. Phased, so you see value in weeks. AI once there is something real to point it at.

Want the readiness scorecard? Reply Y and it is yours.

messages.subject: readiness scorecard
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no call needed.

The scorecard rates {{company}} on the 10 things that have to be true before AI is production-grade, mapped against what you run today.

Should I send it to you, or is {{data_leader_name}} the right person for data and analytics?

messages.subject: engineering at {{company}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, we work with a lot of {{vertical}} companies, and the pattern is almost always the same: the roadmap is ambitious and engineering is the bottleneck, usually spread across a few freelancers and vendors with nobody owning the whole.

We are the one partner that owns it from idea to production. No handoffs, no finger-pointing.

Reply Y and I'll send a short benchmark of how {{vertical}} leaders have modernized. Free.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the reason the freelancer-and-vendor setup breaks is ownership.

When something goes wrong, everyone points at everyone else and you are the integrator.

One accountable partner removes that entirely. We own the outcome.

Want the {{vertical}} benchmark? Reply Y.

messages.subject: benchmark for {{company}}
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no need for a call.

The benchmark shows how the top {{vertical}} companies have set up engineering and data, and the gaps most of the field still has.

Should I send it to you, or whoever owns technology at {{company}}?

launchPlan: Stand up Apollo, LinkedIn Jobs, BuiltWith, and Crunchbase/PitchBook pipelines. Validate the vertical gate against manual samples.
Warm domains and inboxes in parallel for two weeks.
Launch Tier 1 and the Eyecare / Optical lookalike side campaign together. Tier 1 gives the fastest learning loop; eyecare has the strongest proof.
Launch Tier 2 once Tier 1's copy winner is clear. Reserve hand-built blueprints for high-ACV accounts.
Launch Tier 3, then Tier 4 to keep the funnel full.
At week 12, judge T1 and T2 on pipeline value, judge T3 and T4 on reply ratio, and promote the best side campaigns.

thresholds.action: Borderline. Swap lead magnet or notice line.
Pause and rework. For high-ACV T1/T2, judge pipeline before cutting.
